taxonomy.note: First species-specific Salmonidae entry. Genus overview at degree 236.

biology.size: 60-120 cm, 4.5-20 kg typical; record 44.2 kg (Kenai River 1985, 97.5 lbs)
run_types: spring Chinook (springers): Feb-Jul entry, Aug-Sep spawn, fat-rich, most endangered
fall Chinook: Aug-Oct entry, fall spawn, more numerous
juvenile_types: ocean-type (subyearlings): coastal rivers, migrate in first year
stream-type (yearlings): interior rivers, rear 1 year in freshwater
biology.ocean_range: California to Alaska to Bering Sea

sound_profile.description: Waterfall leap: launch splash (low-frequency water displacement) + body-slap on reentry (broadband impact 50-2000+ Hz). Heavier than smaller salmon species due to greater mass. E=6 at active falls during run.
sound_profile.notes: E=6 vs. E=5 genus level (236): Chinook mass generates heavier acoustic impact than smaller species

orca_nexus.dietary_dependency: 80-90%+ Chinook in SRKW diet. Cultural learning -- passed mother to calf for generations.
orca_nexus.conservation_link: SRKW cannot recover without Chinook recovery. Same crisis, two species.

esa_listings: Snake River fall Chinook ESU: Threatened
Snake River spring/summer Chinook ESU: Threatened
Upper Columbia spring Chinook ESU: Endangered
conservation.recovery_success: Elwha dam removal 2011-2014: Chinook documented above former dam sites within a decade

cultural.first_salmon: Chinook is the primary First Salmon ceremony species for most PNW nations
cultural.etymology: Chinook name derives from Chinook Nation of lower Columbia River
cultural.celilo_falls: Most important salmon fishing site in the world before inundation by The Dalles Dam 1957
cultural.boldt_decision: 1974: tribal treaty rights to 50% of harvestable salmon; Chinook as primary subject

theory_nodes.concept: Chinook waterfall leap: E=6 broadband impact. Largest Pacific salmon (to 50 kg) produces heaviest acoustic impacts at falls. Mass-velocity relationship: heavier fish at same velocity = more acoustic energy.
theory_nodes.description: Physics of the King Salmon leap.

theory_nodes.concept: Chinook-Orca nexus: Southern Resident Orca are Chinook-obligate (80-90% diet). SRKW recovery requires Chinook recovery. Same conservation crisis, two species. Cannot be decoupled.
theory_nodes.description: The most critical predator-prey dependency in Salish Sea conservation.

theory_nodes.concept: Two run types: spring Chinook (fat-rich, Feb-Jul entry, most endangered by warming water) and fall Chinook (more numerous, Aug-Oct). Different ecology, different conservation status, same species.
theory_nodes.description: Spring vs. fall Chinook life history divergence.

cross_references.relationship: FIRST SALMONIDAE at genus level. Chinook introduced in five-species overview. Degree 237 is species-specific deep dive.
Next species in Salmonidae chapter. Coho aggressive male spawning vs. Chinook waterfall leap. Different sound, different energy, same family.

s36_pair.pairing_note: King Salmon + king of post-grunge. Both largest in their category. Chinook leap E=6; Foo Fighters E=7. Forward momentum against resistance. E=13 combined.
